what: The smallest field: prepare, then do
title: Coffee Field Model: One Field, One Policy, One Run
text: full coffee process, whole idea miniature — two actions gate . Many plans step whose job make later step work: must prepare ground pays . smallest field captures two actions, Prep , tied together single rule — ’s effect gated Prep produces: Prep produces state Ready — preparation. produces outcome Done, Ready . Written afforded force Done ~ Ready:, → Done push zero Prep set Ready. Done agent values (L ~ Done). afforded force Done ~ Ready:gate; use term lets consume Ready (done thing, preparation spent run settles). policy two rules — prepare agent’s intention, ready: policy wrapped planF() object: carries action ~ condition rules pins ordered step label (a1, a2) short description, diagram writes corner plan-list. planF stands raw policy string anywhere engine takes one — simulateF(). Prep fires first (trigger choice ), setting Ready turn 1; makes eligible, turn 2 produces Done — spending Ready goes — L reaches 1. Now skip preparation reach straight : fire — Ready never set, gated force stays shut, Done never moves, L stays 0. without preparing empty motion. gap whole point primitive, coffee pipeline just shape repeated four times . Reading diagram. Three colour languages run side side, kept apart never blur. Two live forces, one extra colour ever added node: green = action plan’s forces (“green means go”) — policy edge fires action, step label (planF numbers a1, a2). action nodes stay value scale. whole plan also written plan-list upper-left corner, step arrow’s green, step currently firing shown bold. gold = condition — state gates later force. condition one place node takes non-value colour: gating state, force opens, force’s label share gold. force light grey gate shut (potential path) turns gold instant gate opens. red–white–blue = value every node — actions, outcome done, L — white zero, blue fill. diagram reads staircase: policy edge runs level (condition action fires), action’s force climbs diagonally next state; L sits back starting level, diagram closes began. Step ← Back / Forward → watch → done force: grey start (needs ready), gold moment prep sets ready, fires, done lights, ready spent — gate closing behind .
code: library(funfield)  pd_field <- labelF("   Ready ~ 1 * Prep      + use * Ready:Do   Done  ~ 1 * Ready:Do   L     ~ 1 * Done ", actions = c("Prep", "Do")) pd_field$params["use"] <- -1  cat(pd_field$model) #> Ready ~ f1_Prep.Ready * Prep + use * Ready:Do #> Done ~ fReady_Do.Done * Ready:Do #> L ~ f1_Done.L * Done pd_plan <- planF("   Prep ~ choice   # ready the ground   Do   ~ Ready    # do the thing, once ready ", label = "A") pd_plan #> Plan A #>  step rule          description              #>  a1   Prep ~ choice ready the ground         #>  a2   Do ~ Ready    do the thing, once ready  pd_s0 <- c(choice = 1, Prep = 0, Ready = 0, Do = 0, Done = 0, L = 0)  pd_out <- simulateF(pd_field$model, pd_field$params, pd_s0, pd_plan,                     flows = "choice") pd_out$fired #> [1] "Prep" "Do" round(pd_out$trajectory, 2) #>     choice Prep Ready Do Done L #> t=0      1    0     0  0    0 0 #> t=1      0    1     1  0    0 0 #> t=2      0    0     0  1    1 1 pd_skip <- simulateF(pd_field$model, pd_field$params, pd_s0,                      "Do ~ 1 * choice", flows = "choice") pd_skip$fired #> [1] "Do" pd_skip$trajectory[, "L"] #> t=0 t=1  #>   0   0 pd_model  <- paste(pd_field$model, pd_plan$policy, sep = "\n") pd_layout <- data.frame(   name  = c("choice", "Prep", "Ready", "Do", "Done", "L"),   x     = c(0,        2,      3,       5,    6,      8),   y     = c(0,        0,      2,       2,    4,      0),   shape = c("circle", "rtTri", "diamond", "rtTri", "diamond", "lfTri"),   label = c("choice", "prep", "ready", "do", "done", "L"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE)  pd_steps  <- nrow(pd_out$trajectory) - 1L pd_frames <- lapply(0:pd_steps, function(t) {   plotField(pd_model, pd_field$params, pd_out$trajectory[paste0("t=", t), ],             pd_layout, plan = pd_plan, plan_ramp = TRUE,             condition_ramp = TRUE, title = paste("t =", t)) })  plotsAsWidget(pd_frames, panel_titles = paste("Step", 0:pd_steps),               width = 7, height = 3.6)

title: Coffee Field Model: One Field, One Policy, One Run
text: stage coffee process afforded force: path contributes nothing prerequisite state . fZ_X.Y schema term moderator baseline — fZ_X.Y * X:Z — X → Y force zero whenever s[Z] = 0 switches s[Z] turns positive. physical chain afforded forces: real process also uses things : machine brewed, set-rig (s2) spent; pour, coffee leaves pot. write consumption outflow term shared negative coefficient use, e.g. use * s2:TurnOn (“turning consumes rig”). Production consumption together make every object state balance: rises action produces falls next action draws . Two forces close field: value cost. terminal node L — likelihood. ESJT data L rated likelihood taking action; plays role economist give subjective expected utility. Value enters forces L, give two: vector f[., L] — every force L — agent’s values, direct utility function. keep normalised absolute weights sum one (.9 + .1 = 1), reading budget agent cares . Cost enters new node, Energy. Every action carries performance cost: something — even something futile — spends effort. costF() writes us, flat decrement Energy per action, charged bare action node paid whenever action fires, whether action accomplishes anything. Pouring empty pot still costs effort. labelF() gives every fixed force canonical fZ_X.Y label captures value; one parameter leaves us shared use label, consumption strength, set -1. Note : make, TurnOn, Pour Sip ever appears left-hand side. Actions exogenous — field says action ; policy, next, decides one actually fires.
code: library(funfield)  actions <- c("make", "TurnOn", "Pour", "Sip")  afford <- "   s2     ~ 1 * make        + use * s2:TurnOn   HCoPot ~ 1 * s2:TurnOn   + use * HCoPot:Pour   HCoCup ~ 1 * HCoPot:Pour + use * HCoCup:Sip   HCo    ~ 1 * HCoCup:Sip " cost  <- costF(actions, cost = -0.1)  field <- labelF(paste(afford, cost,                       "L ~ 0.9 * HCo + 0.1 * Energy", sep = "\n"),                 actions = actions) field$params["use"] <- -1          # consumption strength (the shared label)  cat(field$model) #> s2 ~ f1_make.s2 * make + use * s2:TurnOn #> HCoPot ~ fs2_TurnOn.HCoPot * s2:TurnOn + use * HCoPot:Pour #> HCoCup ~ fHCoPot_Pour.HCoCup * HCoPot:Pour + use * HCoCup:Sip #> HCo ~ fHCoCup_Sip.HCo * HCoCup:Sip #> Energy ~ f1_make.Energy * make + f1_TurnOn.Energy * TurnOn + f1_Pour.Energy * Pour + f1_Sip.Energy * Sip #> L ~ f1_HCo.L * HCo + f1_Energy.L * Energy field$params #>          f1_make.s2                 use   fs2_TurnOn.HCoPot fHCoPot_Pour.HCoCup  #>                 1.0                -1.0                 1.0                 1.0  #>     fHCoCup_Sip.HCo      f1_make.Energy    f1_TurnOn.Energy      f1_Pour.Energy  #>                 1.0                -0.1                -0.1                -0.1  #>       f1_Sip.Energy            f1_HCo.L         f1_Energy.L  #>                -0.1                 0.9                 0.1

code: planA <- planF("   make   ~ choice    # prepare coffee machine   TurnOn ~ s2        # once prepared, turn on   Pour   ~ HCoPot    # when the pot fills, pour over the cup   Sip    ~ HCoCup    # once the cup is full, take a sip ", label = "A") planA #> Plan A #>  step rule          description                           #>  a1   make ~ choice prepare coffee machine                #>  a2   TurnOn ~ s2   once prepared, turn on                #>  a3   Pour ~ HCoPot when the pot fills, pour over the cup #>  a4   Sip ~ HCoCup  once the cup is full, take a sip planB <- planF("   make   ~ choice    # prepare coffee machine   TurnOn ~ s2        # once prepared, turn on   Sip    ~ HCoCup    # reach for a sip — but the pour never happened ", label = "B")

what: Step 8: Unpacking the conjunction into a process
title: Coffee Field Model: One Field, One Policy, One Run
text: Step 7 compressed whole coffee maker single conjunction: five conditions met, Prepped fires, one TurnOnCM step turns prepped machine pot coffee. drip machine check five boxes brew single motion — runs sequence, condition enters story stage. Power heats plate; hot plate boils water; hot water wets grounds; steeped slurry drips filter; drips collect pot. single turn -> pot coffee step little process, can blow way blew prep — except time five conditions interchangeable, ordered gates along chain. stage internal action machine: prior machine-state drives action , gated relevant condition, produces next machine-state empties one — [state -> action -> state], one rung per turn. machine’s moves rather agent’s, cost effort; agent’s one overt act pressing button. Two choices make chain self-limiting without one-shot token. Brewing gated full conjunction (TurnOnCM ~ Prepped), heating consumes water — boils reservoir machine (use * HasWater:heatW). moment heatW fires water gone, Prepped longer satisfied, button start second brew. aux = prepped3$aux marks conjunction nodes derived; machine states ordinary latching stocks, emptied next internal action. five preps go first, order, TurnOnCM heats plate. machine climbs — heatW, wetG, drip, fill, one rung per turn — run halts moment coffee reaches pot, ten turns . Watch HasWater Prepped: fall 0 heating step, button never fires twice. (button’s energy cost helps : one turn button heatW eligible, free machine step yields slightly higher L, machine advances rather re-brewing.) Drawn, single brew arrow Step 7 becomes staircase. left fan kept exactly — adds still feed five conditions, still converge prepped — prepped now production work: individual conditions open staircase forces. Two edge styles carry climb, convention worth stating general diagrams: condition -> action edge (plan saying “now may act”) runs level, green; action -> condition edge (force act exerts world) runs diagonally , gold--amber condition gates . Read treads risers, machine becomes literal flight stairs. conditions, ordered bottom--top place process, light riser amber; prepped sits inert elbow, blue five hold white instant water spent. long dashed arc curving heat back water water leaving — one consumption , breaking conjunction, keeps brew single pot. first pass expansion: machine’s ingredients yet spent way water , process model faithful shape full bookkeeping — thread pull later.

text: Deviate Level-1 columns within (person, situation) devPSI(), merge condition variable code manager dummy (1 = coworker manager, 0 = coworker subordinate).
code: mediators <- c("Appropriate","MajorArg","GoodRelationP",                "Punished","CompleteJob","P_Punished","CompanySuccess")  ## devPSI() deviates each action's outcome ratings within (person, situation): ## each rating is centered on the person's mean across the actions they rated ## in that same situation, isolating the within-situation action contrast. dat <- merge(devPSI(coworkerESJT$PSI), coworkerESJT$cond, by = c("p","s")) dat$manager <- as.numeric(dat$condition == "manager")

text: Actions score higher ExpDisapproval (.e., actions constitute direct expression disapproval) also seen likely produce major argument/confrontation (MajorArg, F1[X,M] ≈ 0.79) less likely maintain good working relationship long run (GoodRelationP, F1[X,M] ≈ −0.64). also seen appropriate within role (Appropriate, F1[X,M] > 0) likely result coworker formally punished (P_Punished, F1[X,M] > 0).

title: Notation: F, F_t, and the fZ_X.Y schema
text: Every estimable parameter F carries label form 𝚏𝚉_𝚇.𝚈 \boxed{\texttt{fZ\_X.Y}} Read : f-convention coefficient force X Y, moderator Z. schema universal — every parameter fits template — regular two ways: baseline (unmoderated) coefficient special case Z = 1, written f1_X.Y. “interaction X constant 1” — .e., part force fires regardless situation. keep 1 explicit; bare f_X.Y. Multiple moderators cell written multiple fZ_X.Y terms different Z. built-limit many cell can carry. separators load-bearing: _ separates moderator path, . separates source target within path. _ . valid R identifier characters, lavaan parses labels intact (verified lavaan 0.6-21). Generic single-letter slots drop separator. source target one-letter schematic roles X, M, Y — always inductive side, pathXMY() fits generic X → M → Y template — . unnecessary keys written without : f1_XM, fZ_MY, fZ_XY (read two trailing letters source target). dotted form reserved named nodes, deductive side (fS2_TurnOn.HCoPot), multi-character names otherwise run together. forms render matrix-cell display, F[src,tgt] — fZ_XM FZ[X,M] parameter, one lavaan-safe key, reading aid (see F1 / FZ matrix overlay pathXMY_to_F()).

title: Notation: F, F_t, and the fZ_X.Y schema

F_t[X, Y] = f1\_X.Y \;+\; fZ\_X.Y \cdot s_t[Z]. Read propagation step: contribution source X target Y time t s_t[X] · F_t[X, Y]. Y’s update, summing sources, st+1[Y]=∑Xst[X]⋅Ft[X,Y]. s_{t+1}[Y] \;=\; \sum_X s_t[X] \cdot F_t[X, Y].

what: Expectation paths (F1[X,M]): what does agreeing to overtime produce?
title: Moderated mediation with `pathXMY()`: the work-overtime EXSJT
text: Expectation paths (Overtime -> M) Agreeing work overtime expected substantially increase Drained (+0.32), RelationshipQuality asker (+0.28), RaisePromotion (+0.21), EmployerValuation (+0.16). expected reduce OutsideRelationships (−0.16) likelihood Punished company (−0.16). Smaller positive expectations attach CompanyHealth, Finances, Appropriate.
code: knitr::kable(   subset(norm$tidy_loop, param == "f1_XM")[, c("mediator","est","se","z","pvalue")],   digits = 3,   caption = "Expectation paths (Overtime -> M)" )

what: Valuation paths (F1[M,Y]): which expectations drive action likelihood?
title: Moderated mediation with `pathXMY()`: the work-overtime EXSJT
text: Valuation paths (M -> Likelihood) Appropriate (+0.90) Finances (+0.89) strongest positive drivers taking action. RaisePromotion (+0.65), EmployerValuation (+0.60), RelationshipQuality (+0.57) also strongly increase likelihood. Drained (−0.33) Punished (−0.35) suppress likelihood. valuation pattern intuitive: financial, evaluative, relational gains pull respondents toward action; expected fatigue punishment push away.
code: knitr::kable(   subset(norm$tidy_loop, param == "f1_MY")[, c("mediator","est","se","z","pvalue")],   digits = 3,   caption = "Valuation paths (M -> Likelihood)" )

title: Moderated mediation with `pathXMY()`: the work-overtime EXSJT
text: Indirect effects mediator pull toward agreeing comes mainly expected gains RelationshipQuality (+0.16), RaisePromotion (+0.14), Finances (+0.11), EmployerValuation (+0.10). push toward declining comes expected Drained (−0.10) harm OutsideRelationships (−0.06).
code: knitr::kable(   subset(norm$tidy_loop, param == "f1_XM * f1_MY")[, c("mediator","est","se","z","pvalue")],   digits = 3,   caption = "Indirect effects through each mediator" )

title: Moderated mediation with `pathXMY()`: the work-overtime EXSJT

code: cot <- overtimeESJT$cond[, c("p","cashReward")] cot$paid <- as.numeric(cot$cashReward ==   "the company will pay you overtime (above your normal pay rate) to work on the project") dat <- merge(dat, cot[, c("p","paid")], by = "p")  mod_s <- pathXMY(dat, X = "Overtime", Y = "Likelihood",                  M = mediators, Z = "paid", Z.within = FALSE) dec_paid <- pathXMY_decompose(dat, X = "Overtime", Y = "Likelihood",                               M = mediators, Z = "paid", Z.within = FALSE)

what: Can we identify reasons that paying for overtime increases the likelihood of agreeing to work overtime?
title: Moderated mediation with `pathXMY()`: the work-overtime EXSJT
text: Pay status’s effect overall agreement decision routes expected outcomes get shifted (expectation arm, FZ[X,M]) expected outcome weighted choice (valuation arm, FZ[M,Y]). pretty pairtable lays two arms side side: expectation arm, six nine outcomes significantly moderated. largest shifts Appropriate (+0.141, p < .001) Finances (+0.121, p < .001) — agreeing paid overtime feels role-appropriate genuinely produces money, large margin. Smaller significant positive shifts attach EmployerValuation, OutsideRelationships, RelationshipQuality, RaisePromotion. cost-side outcomes — Drained, Punished, CompanyHealth — untouched: pay status doesn’t change depleting risky people expect overtime , shifts upside action. valuation arm, one path reaches p < .05: Drained (+0.190, p = .02). positive sign means average negative weight fatigue carries agreement decision (F1[M,Y] ≈ −0.33) less negative overtime paid — tired matters less choice ’s money table. Two cost paths near-miss significance direction (Punished, p = .08; OutsideRelationships, p = .08). speeding fits, valuation-arm standard errors run roughly 5× expectation-arm SEs (design-variance asymmetry: X guaranteed within-person variation, mediators don’t always follow), magnitude-equivalent effects right side held weaker statistical claim left.
code: group_kable(   pathXMY_pairtable(mod_s, c("fZ_XM", "fZ_MY")),   groups = c(" " = 1,              "Pay-status moderation: expectation arm (FZ[X,M])" = 4,              "Pay-status moderation: valuation arm (FZ[M,Y])"   = 4),   col_labels = c("Mediator", "est", "se", "z", "p",                              "est", "se", "z", "p") )

what: Do those shifts create reasons to agree?
title: Moderated mediation with `pathXMY()`: the work-overtime EXSJT
text: expectation arm valuation arm become reasons paired counterpart side. expectancy shift Appropriate reason Appropriate carries weight Likelihood; valuation shift Drained reason overtime actually moves Drained. plotPathXMY_routes() shows two products directly: expectation route pairs green FZ[X,M](paid) shift surviving black average F1[M,Y]; valuation route pairs black average F1[X,M] green FZ[M,Y](paid). panels read left--right: complete chain arms visibly thick candidate reason paying shifts agreement.  expectation route work. strongest reason runs Appropriate (+0.099, p < .001) — agreeing paid overtime feels appropriate degree agreeing unpaid overtime , appropriateness turn strongly drives action. second-largest reason Finances (+0.054, p < .001): paid overtime expected actually produce financial gain (’s definition condition), financial gain weighs heavily choice. Smaller real expectation-route reasons run EmployerValuation (+0.022, p = .01) RaisePromotion (+0.016, p = .02), RelationshipQuality OutsideRelationships borderline. valuation route contributes one significant reason: Drained (+0.060, p = .02). Overtime reliably expected produce fatigue (F1[X,M](Drained) ≈ +0.32 normatively); fatigue’s weight decision shrinks paid conditions, shrinkage acts route toward agreement even though underlying expectation didn’t change. Punished OutsideRelationships carry borderline valuation-route reasons direction (paid, threat punishment threat outside relationships matter slightly less choice). composite picture: paying overtime increases agreement mostly changing respondents expect action produce — primarily appropriate agree, agreement actually financially rewarding — secondarily softening weight placed one cost action reliably produces (fatigue). upsides get amplified one downsides gets discounted; rest cost ledger stays .

code: screen <- screenModerators(psi    = dat,                            X      = "Overtime",                            Y      = "Likelihood",                            M      = mediators,                            traits = overtimeESJT$traits) #> screenModerators: skipping non-numeric trait columns: employment, education, income, ethnicity, gender  labels <- overtimeESJT$codebook$trait_items[, c("var","label")] screen <- merge(screen, labels,                 by.x = "trait", by.y = "var", all.x = TRUE, sort = FALSE)  screen_yx <- subset(screen, target == "YX") screen_yx <- screen_yx[order(-abs(screen_yx$beta)), ] knitr::kable(head(screen_yx, 15)[, c("trait","r","beta","p","label")],              digits = 3, row.names = FALSE,              caption = paste0("Top 15 traits ranked by the within-person ",                               "Overtime effect on Likelihood: a fast screen ",                               "for total-moderation candidates."))

code: ## Local helpers for the three moderation schematics below. Wrap a ## fragment in gold to flag it as an FZ coefficient, and in light ## gray to fade it into "not part of this term". ## Note: starred-total cells use * (HTML numeric entity for `*`) ## rather than a literal asterisk -- gridtext treats paired `*` ## characters as Markdown italic, which would break color spans that ## happen to bracket them. gold <- "#F6BE00"; gray <- "#E5E5E5" gz <- function(s) paste0("", s, "") gr <- function(s) paste0("", s, "") f1_XM <- "F1[X,M]" f1_MY <- "F1[M,Y]" f1_XY <- "F1[X,Y]" fZ_XM <- "FZ[X,M]" fZ_MY <- "FZ[M,Y]" fZ_XY <- "FZ[X,Y]" f1s_XY <- "F1*[X,Y]" # starred F1 (total) fZs_XY <- "FZ*[X,Y]" # starred FZ (total) plotPathSchema( XY_label = paste0(f1s_XY, " ", gz(paste0("+ ", fZs_XY))), X_shape = "rtTri", Y_shape = "lfTri" ) plotPathSchema( M_label = "M", XM_label = paste0(f1_XM, " ", gz(paste0("+ ", fZ_XM))), MY_label = paste0(f1_MY, " ", gz(paste0("+ ", fZ_MY))), XY_label = paste0(f1_XY, " ", gz(paste0("+ ", fZ_XY))), X_shape = "rtTri", Y_shape = "lfTri" ) ## Helper: stitch a single XY-label string for an arm where one of ## (f1, fZ) is active and the other is faded. arm_lab <- function(f1, fZ, active = c("f1", "fZ")) { active <- match.arg(active) if (active == "f1") paste0(f1, " ", gr(paste0("+ ", fZ))) else paste0(gr(f1), " ", gz(paste0("+ ", fZ))) } ## And a fully faded label (whole "f1 + fZ" string wrapped in gray). arm_off <- function(f1, fZ) gr(paste0(f1, " + ", fZ)) ## Panel 1: FZ[X,M]·F1[M,Y] (expectation-route moderation). p1 <- plotPathSchema( M_label = "M", XM_label = arm_lab(f1_XM, fZ_XM, "fZ"), # fZ active -> green line MY_label = arm_lab(f1_MY, fZ_MY, "f1"), # f1 active -> black line XY_label = arm_off(f1_XY, fZ_XY), # not in this term X_shape = "rtTri", Y_shape = "lfTri", XM_color = gold, MY_color = "black", XY_color = gray, title = "Term 1: FZ[X,M] · F1[M,Y]" ) + ggplot2::theme(plot.title = ggtext::element_markdown(hjust = 0.5)) ## Panel 2: F1[X,M]·FZ[M,Y] (valuation-route moderation). p2 <- plotPathSchema( M_label = "M", XM_label = arm_lab(f1_XM, fZ_XM, "f1"), MY_label = arm_lab(f1_MY, fZ_MY, "fZ"), XY_label = arm_off(f1_XY, fZ_XY), X_shape = "rtTri", Y_shape = "lfTri", XM_color = "black", MY_color = gold, XY_color = gray, title = "Term 2: F1[X,M] · FZ[M,Y]" ) + ggplot2::theme(plot.title = ggtext::element_markdown(hjust = 0.5)) ## Panel 3: FZ[X,Y] (residual direct moderation). p3 <- plotPathSchema( M_label = "M", XM_label = arm_off(f1_XM, fZ_XM), MY_label = arm_off(f1_MY, fZ_MY), XY_label = arm_lab(f1_XY, fZ_XY, "fZ"), X_shape = "rtTri", Y_shape = "lfTri", XM_color = gray, MY_color = gray, XY_color = gold, title = "Term 3: FZ[X,Y]" ) + ggplot2::theme(plot.title = ggtext::element_markdown(hjust = 0.5)) patchwork::wrap_plots(p1, p2, p3, ncol = 3) ## Same three frames as Figure 3, but stitched into a Back/Forward ## widget so they swap at pixel-identical layout instead of sitting ## side by side. p1/p2/p3 are reused from the chunk above. plotsAsWidget( list(p1, p2, p3), panel_titles = c( "Term 1: FZ[X,M]·F1[M,Y] (expectation route)", "Term 2: F1[X,M]·FZ[M,Y] (valuation route)", "Term 3: FZ[X,Y] (residual direct)" ), width = 7, height = 3.5, format = "svg" )

Error      t value     Pr(>|t|)  #> 2.536665e-01 2.534038e-02 1.001037e+01 1.245500e-20  ## (A') what screenModerators() actually calls -- Pearson cor.test on ## the same Δ vector. The `estimate` it returns is `r` (the ## standardized form of b₂; SD(dL) cancels into SD-units) but the ## *t*-statistic and *p*-value are the same hypothesis test. ct <- cor.test(dL, SRz) c(r = unname(ct$estimate), t = unname(ct$statistic), p = ct$p.value) #>            r            t            p  #> 4.936504e-01 1.001037e+01 1.245500e-20  ## (B) same numbers from person fixed effects on the 2N-row form coef(summary(lm(Likelihood ~ Speed * SRz + factor(p),                 data = d2)))["Speed:SRz", ] #>     Estimate   Std. title: Coworker Assertiveness ESJT Dataset — coworkerESJT
text: Data cleaning. raw dataset contained 296 participants. Three sequential screens applied: Straightlining (fancyr::strLine()): 42 participants     removed zero-variance expected-outcome ratings 10%     action ratings. Names left-hand side ignored. Default `character(0)` — every node recomputes (memoryless), matching field's afforded-equilibrium behaviour. flows Character vector **exogenous** node names (never left-hand side) treat one-shot pulses: seeded, read, zeroed step instead carried forward. Endogenous nodes already recompute unaffected. Default `character(0)`. Requires field DAG; feedback cycle raises     error (use `"sync"` instead). `"sync"` One discrete time step stock--flow system,     evaluated standard rate--integrate order tick     advances process one stage. Three phases run sequence:     (1) **rate flows** — non-stock, non-readout nodes (typically     *actions*) — recomputed start--step **stock     levels**; (2) **stocks** integrate, `new = old + inflow - outflow`,     reading *fresh* rate flows just computed *old* stock     levels; (3) **readouts** (named `readouts`) recompute     *freshly updated* stocks. Evaluating rates integrating means     stock consumed tick action draws     fires, affording state lives exactly one step action     fires exactly **** trigger spent.     natural mode cyclic / dynamical fields — particular     stock--flow consumption pipelines, inherently cyclic     sweep. **Stocks vs. flows.** default every endogenous node recomputed scratch step (*flow*: value pure function current state, persist ). node named `stocks` instead *latches*: carries previous value forward model's terms add subtract , .e. `s_(t+1)[Y] = s_t[Y] + sum_k coef_k * prod(...)`. Stocks field remembers — object state stays attained, negative (consumption) term `c * Y:trigger` draws back . lavaan forbids self-edge `Y ~ Y`, carry-forward written model string; marking `Y` stock supplies . See [simulateF()], drives one-step propagator turn-based process derives stock set automatically, `vignette("coffee_field_model", package = "funfield")` worked stock/flow example. title: Auto-generate `fZ_X.Y` parameter labels for a field model — labelF
text: Takes `lavaan`-syntax field model whose regression terms may unlabelled (e.g. `Y ~ X:Z`) carry fixed coefficients (e.g. `Y ~ 1 * X:Z`) rewrites every term canonical `fZ_X.Y` label, models can specified without typing labels hand. Returns relabelled model string `params` vector pre-populated coefficients input fixed.

title: Auto-generate `fZ_X.Y` parameter labels for a field model — labelF
text: model `lavaan`-syntax model string regression (`~`) rows. actions Optional character vector action (source) variable names. supplied, warning raised term action appears condition position, since convention `Y ~ Condition:Action`. Default `NULL` (check). default Value assigned `params` terms neither carry fixed coefficient existing label--value. Default `NA`.

title: Auto-generate `fZ_X.Y` parameter labels for a field model — labelF
text: list two elements: `model` relabelled model string: every regression term       rewritten `label * rhs`. `params` named numeric vector, one entry per generated /       retained label, holding fixed value input       supplied one `default` otherwise. Suitable passing straight [simulateF()] / [evalF()] (  `$model`, `$params`), stitching two halves   `paste($model, b$model)` `c($params, b$params)`.

title: Auto-generate `fZ_X.Y` parameter labels for a field model — labelF
text: Labelling **order-dependent**, order read straight right-hand side: `Y ~ Z:X` becomes `fZ_X.Y`. interaction written **condition first, action last** — leading variable(s) gating **condition** `Z`, final variable **action** / source `X`, `Y` **target**. generic form `Y ~ Condition:Action`, generating `fCondition_Action.Y`. term single right-hand variable (`Y ~ X`) carries condition becomes baseline `f1_X.Y`; multiple leading conditions concatenated (matching compound `fab_X.Y` convention). Variable names used verbatim (case change), labels round-trip cleanly. order load-bearing, conditions must precede actions. `actions` supplied, `labelF()` warns one action variables turns condition (non-final) position — sign term written `Y ~ Action:Condition` mistake. fixed coefficient (`1 * Z:X`, `c * Z:X`) ** obstacle: label generated exactly free term, fixed value captured parameter's entry `params`. example, row `s2 ~ 1 * s1:make` becomes `s2 ~ fs1_make.s2 * s1:make` `params["fs1_make.s2"] = 1`. Terms left free unfixed get `params` value `default` (`NA` default), filled model run. Terms already carry label keep .

what: General Path / SEM Field Models with Optional Moderation — pathF
title: General Path / SEM Field Models with Optional Moderation — pathF
text: Fits arbitrary recursive path (SEM) model PSI long-format data cluster-robust standard errors, generalizing pathXMY single \(X \M \Y\) triangle cascades \(X \M_1 \M_2 \\dots \Y\) arbitrary directed acyclic path structures. structural part can supplied two ways: order: character vector variables presumed         causal order. auto-expands saturated         lower-triangular cascade every causally upstream variable         predicts every downstream variable         (M1 ~ X; M2 ~ X + M1; M3 ~ X + M1 + M2;         Y ~ X + M1 + M2 + M3). paths: character vector lavaan-style regression         formulas (e.g. c("Y ~ M2", "M2 ~ X + M1", "M1 ~ X"))         arbitrary DAG. Supply exactly one order paths. moderator Z given, every structural edge \(b\,\) expanded \(f_1 + f_Z (\times Z)\) Z main effect added endogenous equation, exactly pathXMY(). Indirect effects enumerated along every directed \(X \Y\) path two edges, product edge coefficients, plus (moderation) first-order route-moderation terms.

title: General Path / SEM Field Models with Optional Moderation — pathF
code: pathF(   data,   order = NULL,   paths = NULL,   X = NULL,   Y = NULL,   Z = NULL,   Z.within = FALSE,   cluster = "p",   situation = "s",   controls = NULL,   deviate = c("auto", "none"),   conf.level = 0.95,   check.deviation = TRUE,   suppress.warnings = TRUE,   max_paths = 256L )

title: General Path / SEM Field Models with Optional Moderation — pathF
text: data data frame PSI long format (p, s, first columns). cascade/path variables within-(person, situation) deviated automatically (see deviate); Z handled separately. order Optional character vector variable names presumed causal order. Expands saturated lower-triangular cascade. Supply paths, . paths Optional character vector lavaan-style regression formulas ("lhs ~ rhs1 + rhs2 + ...") describing arbitrary DAG. right-hand-side term r contributes directed edge r -> lhs. X, Y Optional names focal initiating action outcome used enumerate indirect-effect routes. Default first last element order; paths, default unique source / sink node one exists (otherwise indirect enumeration skipped unless supplied). Z Optional moderator (length-1 character). NULL, unmoderated model fit. Z.within Logical (default FALSE). TRUE, Z within-cluster deviated (situation-level moderator); FALSE z-standardized globally (-person trait). cluster Name clustering variable (default "p"); set NULL fit without cluster-robust SEs (recommended). situation Name situation column (default "s"), paired cluster automatic within-cell deviation. controls Optional character vector control variables added every endogenous equation. deviate One "auto" (default) "none"; see pathXMY. conf.level Confidence level CI columns (default 0.95). check.deviation Logical (default TRUE); warn cascade variables look within-person deviated. suppress.warnings Logical (default TRUE); muffle cosmetic non-positive-definite vcov warning lavaan emits machine precision. max_paths Integer (default 256). Cap number directed \(X \Y\) paths enumerated indirect effects. saturated \(K\)-node cascade \(2^{K-2}\) paths; cap, indirect definitions skipped warning (structural edges still returned).

title: General Path / SEM Field Models with Optional Moderation — pathF
text: list class "pathF" : tidy tidy data frame, one row per structural edge per       indirect-effect term. Columns: param, src, tgt,       label (internal lavaan parameter label, debugging       $fit), est, se, z,       pvalue, ci.lower, ci.upper. Edge       params use F-schema source-target form       f1_<src>_<tgt> / fZ_<src>_<tgt>; indirect rows use        * -joined product string (e.g.       f1_X_M * f1_M_Y, fZ_X_M * f1_M_Y). fit fitted lavaan object. spec parsed specification (nodes, edges,       X, Y). model generated lavaan model string (useful debugging). data prepared data frame model fit (deviated,       .Z moderator, token alias columns v1..vK,       interaction product columns). Used resampling wrappers.

title: General Path / SEM Field Models with Optional Moderation — pathF
text: saturated recursive path model just-identified algebraically equivalent sequence OLS regressions, lavaan fits without iteration. moderation interaction terms across many edges can highly collinear, inflating standard errors; inspect $model SEs cascade long. 3-variable saturated cascade order = c(X, M, Y) reproduces single-mediator pathXMY() fit exactly: f1_X_M \(=\) f1_XM, f1_X_Y \(=\) f1_XY, f1_M_Y \(=\) f1_MY, indirect/route-moderation products match.

title: General Path / SEM Field Models with Optional Moderation — pathF
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ data(speedingESJT) PSI <- speedingESJT$PSI  ## Saturated causal cascade Speed -> OnTime -> Appropriate -> Likelihood fitc <- pathF(PSI, order = c("Speed", "OnTime", "Appropriate", "Likelihood")) fitc$tidy  ## Same, moderated by a between-person trait sr <- speedingESJT$traits[, c("p", "SR_30")] names(sr)[2] <- "SRFast" dd <- merge(PSI, sr, by = "p") fitm <- pathF(dd, order = c("Speed", "OnTime", "Appropriate", "Likelihood"),               Z = "SRFast") fitm$tidy  ## Arbitrary DAG via formulas pathF(PSI, paths = c("Likelihood ~ Appropriate + OnTime",                      "Appropriate ~ Speed",                      "OnTime ~ Speed"),       X = "Speed", Y = "Likelihood") } # }

what: Decompose total and Z-moderated X-Y effects across a path model's routes — pathF_decompose
title: Decompose total and Z-moderated X-Y effects across a path model's routes — pathF_decompose
text: Generalizes pathXMY_decompose single-mediator triangle arbitrary pathF models. Two models fit: Total model bare \(X \Y\) regression (\(X     \times Z\) moderation Z supplied), giving total     effect \(F_1^{*}[X,Y]\) total moderation \(F_Z^{*}[X,Y]\). Path model full pathF() model specified     order paths, total decomposes across     every directed \(X \Y\) route. baseline identity generalizes Baron-Kenny sum: total \(F_1^{*}[X,Y]\) equals sum routes route's product \(F_1\) edges, plus residual direct \(F_1[X,Y]\) (exact saturated cascade, path model reproduces OLS algebra). moderation identity generalizes Muller-Judd-Yzerbyt (2005) decomposition: route, \(Z\)'s moderation "slides" chain, contributing one first-order term per edge position — moderated edge's \(F_Z\) times \(F_1\) every edge route. two-mediator chain \(X \M_1 \M_2 \Y\): $$F_Z^{*}[X,Y] \approx F_Z[X,M_1] F_1[M_1,M_2] F_1[M_2,Y]   + F_1[X,M_1] F_Z[M_1,M_2] F_1[M_2,Y]   + F_1[X,M_1] F_1[M_1,M_2] F_Z[M_2,Y]   + F_Z[X,Y]$$ summed every route DAG several. identity first-order: substituting moderated equations chain produces \(Z^2, Z^3, \dots\) cross-terms (products two \(F_Z\) coefficients) decomposition drops, approximation can loosen chains lengthen. gap rows output make approximation quality explicit.

what: Path Analysis for X -> M -> Y Structures with Optional Moderation — pathXMY
title: Path Analysis for X -> M -> Y Structures with Optional Moderation — pathXMY
text: Fits path model $$M = \beta^1_{MX} X + \beta^Z_{MX}(X \times Z) + \beta^Z_M Z + e_M$$ $$Y = \beta^1_{YX} X + \beta^Z_{YX}(X \times Z) + \beta^1_{YM} M + \beta^Z_{YM}(M \times Z) + \beta^Z_Y Z + e_Y$$ via lavaan cluster-robust standard errors (clustering cluster, typically person ID "p" ESJT data). Omitting M collapses direct \(X \Y\) model (optionally moderated). Omitting Z collapses unmoderated version. Supplying multiple variable names M loops model mediator one time returns single stacked tidy table.

title: Path Analysis for X -> M -> Y Structures with Optional Moderation — pathXMY
text: data data frame PSI long format (p, s, first columns). default Level-1 columns X, M, Y within-(person, situation) deviated automatically (see deviate); Z handled separately. X Name initiating action / predictor variable (length-1 character). Y Name outcome variable (length-1 character; typically "L" likelihood). M Optional. Name(s) candidate mediator variables (character vector). NULL, direct \(X \Y\) model fit (mediator). length > 1, X-M-Y model fit per mediator. Z Optional. Name moderator variable (length-1 character). NULL, unmoderated model fit. default (Z.within = FALSE), Z treated -person trait z-standardized globally. Set Z.within = TRUE situation-level moderator varies within persons (e.g., experimentally varied condition); case Z within-cluster deviated instead. Z.within Logical (default FALSE). TRUE, Z treated within-cluster (situation-level) variable within-person deviated fitting, exactly like Level-1 predictors. Use moderator varies across situations within person (e.g., randomly assigned scenario condition). FALSE (default), Z z-standardized globally (-person trait interpretation). cluster Name clustering variable (default "p"). Set NULL fit without cluster-robust SEs (recommended ESJT data). situation Name situation/scenario column (default "s"), paired cluster define (person, situation) cells used automatic deviation. controls Optional character vector control variables added equations. deviate One "auto" (default) "none". "auto", X, M, Y within-(person, situation) deviated fitting — value centered mean actions rated (cluster, situation) cell — provided situation column present every cell holds least two rows. cells hold single action data left untouched (deviate first, e.g. devPSI). "none", deviation applied pre-deviated data assumed. se One "cluster" (default; sandwich SEs lavaan) "boot" (cluster bootstrap nboot replicates). nboot Number bootstrap replicates se = "boot". conf.level Confidence level CI columns (default 0.95). check.deviation Logical. TRUE (default), warn X, M, Y still look within-person deviated deviate step — relevant mainly deviate = "none" data pre-deviated. suppress.warnings Logical. TRUE (default), suppress cosmetic non-PD vcov warning lavaan emits machine precision. joint Logical (default TRUE). length(M) > 1, additionally fit single joint multi-mediator model mediators appear simultaneously Y equation. joint fit contributes residual \(X \Y\) path (controlling full mediator set), parallel per-mediator coefficients suffix _joint. See Details.

title: Path Analysis for X -> M -> Y Structures with Optional Moderation — pathXMY
text: Deviation. cluster = "p" lavaan provides cluster-robust sandwich standard errors single-level SEM; multilevel model. Correct within-person path coefficients therefore require Level-1 variables (X, M, Y) deviated within (cluster, situation) cell. default (deviate = "auto") pathXMY() internally; pass deviate = "none" data already deviated. one action rated per (person, situation), within-cell deviation isolates within-situation action contrast field model ; single situation per person reduces ordinary within-person deviation. Z handled separately: -person trait moderator (Z.within = FALSE) z-standardized, situation-level moderator (Z.within = TRUE) within-person deviated. Small cluster counts. cluster-robust z-tests assume number clusters \(G\) large. \(G < 50\), results may anti-conservative; consider se = "boot". Naming convention. Parameters use F-schema source-target convention (see vignette("notation")): f1_* baseline coefficients, fZ_* Z-moderated coefficients; f*_XM parameters regress M X (\(X \M\) force), f*_MY regress Y M (\(M \Y\)), f*_XY (direct) \(X \Y\) coefficients. two trailing letters read source target, matching matrix-cell display form F[src,tgt]. Loop vs joint fits — two different regressions, two different tables. multiple mediators, pathXMY() produces two separate tidy tables, deliberately kept apart answer different questions coefficients interchangeable: $tidy_loop comes "loop" pass: one independent         X-M-Y model fit per mediator,         single mediator Y equation. row labeled f1_MY         mediator \(m_k\) slope Y \(m_k\)         controlling X — nothing else. loop pass         inferential workhorse supports stable         expectation-route summary fZ_XM * f1_MY. $tidy_joint comes single simultaneous regression         mediators appear together Y equation.         row labeled f1_MY_joint mediator \(m_k\)         partial slope Y \(m_k\) net every         mediator M. comparable loop         f1_MY values routinely differ magnitude         (sometimes sign) mediators correlated. f1_XY_joint fZ_XY_joint useful joint outputs: index residual direct \(X \Y\) (Z-moderation) controlling entire mediator set, serve diagnostic whether measured mediators absorb total fZ_XY moderation. Per-mediator joint coefficients (especially fZ_MY_joint) typically less stable loop counterparts mediators numerous correlated, M--Z product terms highly collinear (Wood, Adanu, & Harms, 2025). inference single mediator's role, prefer loop coefficient $tidy_loop; treat joint coefficients $tidy_joint system-level diagnostic.

code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ data(speedingESJT) ## X, M, Y are within-(person, situation) deviated automatically. PSI <- speedingESJT$PSI  ## Unmoderated mediation, one mediator res1 <- pathXMY(PSI, X = "Speed", Y = "Likelihood", M = "Crash") res1$tidy_loop  ## Mediation across all eight outcome features mediators <- c("Crash","Injured","Ticket","MoneyCost","OnTime",                "IntQuality","FunDrive","Appropriate") res2 <- pathXMY(PSI, X = "Speed", Y = "Likelihood", M = mediators) ## Loop pass: per-mediator single-mediator regressions res2$tidy_loop ## Joint pass: simultaneous multi-mediator regression res2$tidy_joint } # }

what: Decompose a Z-moderated X-Y effect into expectation-, valuation-, and direct-moderation components — pathXMY_decompose
title: Decompose a Z-moderated X-Y effect into expectation-, valuation-, and direct-moderation components — pathXMY_decompose
text: moderator Z acting link X Y, two models commonly interest: Total model direct moderation \(Y = F_1[X,Y] X +     F_Z[X,Y] X \cdot Z + \dots\). total fZ_XY     (\(F_Z^{*}[X,Y]\)) indexes total moderation effect —     much \(Z\) shifts action-outcome link, without reference     mediator. Mediated model full mediated model \(X \M \Y\)     \(Z\) moderating three paths. related algebraic identity (applied one mediator time, linear regression cluster-robust SEs): $$F_Z^{*}[X,Y] = F_Z[X,M] \cdot F_1[M,Y] + F_1[X,M] \cdot F_Z[M,Y] + F_Z[X,Y]$$ Term 1, fZ_XM * f1_MY: moderation flows     \(Z\) changing expectation \(M\) given \(X\).     pathXMY() highlights default. Term 2, f1_XM * fZ_MY: moderation flows     \(Z\) changing valuation \(M\) driver     \(Y\), leaving expectations untouched. Term 3, residual fZ_XY: direct \(Z\)-moderation     \(X \Y\) link route mediator. Identifying term carries total fZ_XY given mediator answers different psychological question pathXMY() alone. person-trait moderator may leave expectations untouched (fZ_XM near zero) yet substantially shift valuation (fZ_MY large) — invisible fZ_XM table central understanding trait moderates action.

text: model `lavaan`-syntax model string `fZ_X.Y` labels (optionally fixed `1 * X` terms), consumed [evalF()] / [simulateF()]. `plan` layered onto situation model, pass full stitched string . params Named numeric vector parameter values. s Named numeric vector: state draw field. layout data frame describing node placement, columns `name` (matching model's variables), `x`, `y`, `shape` (one `"square"`, `"diamond"`, `"rtTri"`, `"lfTri"`, `"circle"`). convention objects diamonds, actions right-facing triangles, likelihood readout left-facing triangle, choice / chance node circle. optional `label` column supplies display text (defaults `name`). optional `glyph` column marks node **glyph node**: holds non-empty string, string drawn centred inside shape caption placed beneath . body still value-shaded like node — e.g. `Energy` node drawn square holding lightning bolt reddens energy spent. plan Optional `lavaan`-syntax sub-model string, [planF()] object. Edges whose source -> target identity appears plan take `plan_color` base colour. Typically action-plan half stitched model. `planF` supplied, `$policy` supplies edges, `$prefix` defaults `plan_label`, step descriptions drive corner **plan-list** (see `plan_legend`). Default `NULL` (gold edges). plan_label Optional single-character prefix naming plan (e.g. `""` "Plan "). supplied, plan edge labelled **step** plan – order `action ~ condition` rule appears `plan` – `<plan_label><step>` (Plan 's four rules read `a1`, `a2`, `a3`, `a4`). Default `NULL` (plan edges keep coefficient / condition label). plan_ramp Logical; `TRUE`, **action plan's forces** coloured green ramp ("green means go" cue): plan edge `<plan_label><step>` label take colour stepped along `plan_colors` plan order (first step = ramp's first colour), shown edge's condition met `potential_color` (grey) merely potential. **forces** recoloured — action *nodes* keep value scale (white idle, blue turn fire), plan adds new node colour. `FALSE`, plan edges take flat `plan_color` (gold). Default `FALSE`. plan_colors Anchor colours `plan_ramp`, interpolated one colour per plan step. Default `c("#00FF00", "#32CD32", "#41A317")` (lime -> limegreen -> dark lime green). conjunctive Character vector target node names whose incoming edges **conjunctive** — node fires ** (product, sum). edges drawn **dotted** carry numeric label, mistaken additive regression weights. Default `NULL`. condition_labels Logical; `TRUE` (default), **gated** edge (interaction `Y ~ Z:X`, `Z` condition opens `X -> Y` force) labelled condition's displayed name — e.g. `TurnOn -> HCoPot`, gated `s2`, reads "rig set" — rather coefficient. Ungated paths keep numeric weight. Set `FALSE` label every edge coefficient. condition_ramp Logical; `TRUE`, **condition states** — moderators `Z` gate afforded forces (`Y ~ Z:X`) — coloured gold -> orange ramp instead red-white-blue value scale. condition node, gated force opens, force's label take colour stepped along `condition_colors` condition's order appearance (earliest = ramp's first colour). node fills colour present white absent; gated force label show colour condition met `potential_color` (grey) . Actions non-condition states keep diverging value scale. Default `FALSE`. (`plan_ramp` gives two parallel staircases: green action ramp gold condition ramp, kept clear red-white-blue value scale.) condition_colors Anchor colours `condition_ramp`, interpolated one colour per condition. Default `c("#F6BE00", "#9A6000")` (gold -> deep amber), kept clear value-red green action ramp. edge_color, plan_color Base (fully active) colours situation edges plan-contributed edges — colour path takes condition fully met. Defaults `"black"` `"#DAA520"` (gold). potential_color Colour **potential** path — one drawn model structure whose condition yet met. edge interpolates toward base colour condition's activation goes 0 -> 1. Default `"grey80"` (light legible). fill_limits Numeric length-2 range mapped diverging fill scale. Default `c(-1, 1)`; values outside squished ends. fill_low, fill_mid, fill_high Diverging fill colours low, zero, high ends. Default red `"#b73712"` / white / blue `"#1572da"`, midpoint `0`. na_fill Fill layout nodes absent `s`. Default `"grey90"`. edge_min Minimum absolute coefficient path drawn . Default `1e-9` (draw structural force). edge_scale_max Absolute resolved coefficient mapped full-strength edge (linewidth colour saturate ). Default `1`. curvature Arc curvature backward consumption edges (negative member anti-parallel pair), drawn quadratic Bezier bow clear straight forward edge otherwise overlay. Negative bows arc forward edge (leftward "mirrored smile"); dip deep enough keep two edges' mid-shaft labels colliding. Default `-1`. node_size Half-extent node shape data coordinates. Default `0.32`. label_pad Gap node's perimeter label. Default `0.12`. label_size, edge_label_size Text sizes node edge labels. Defaults `3.2` `3`. glyph_size Text size centred glyph-node symbols. Default `5`. glyph_color Fill colour lightning-bolt glyph (`glyph` `"bolt"` `"⚡"`). Default blue `"#1572da"` (positive-activation colour). edge_linewidth Linewidth full-strength edge (`|f| >= edge_scale_max`); fainter forces scale . Default `2.1`. arrow_inches Arrowhead length inches. Default `0.15`. head sharp, solid (mitred) triangle, size every edge positive negative forces alike. arrow_size Length (data units) solid tip segment carries arrowhead, kept solid head reads cleanly even dashed shaft. Default `0.26`. arrow_stroke Linewidth arrowhead's stroke (constant, shaft's `lw`). Kept thin sharp mitred tip lands exactly node outline instead overshooting . Default `0.8`. arrow_gap Distance (data units) head end pulled back node perimeter, arrowhead tip rests outline rather poking inside. Default `0.06`. hollow_potential Logical; `TRUE` (default), **potential** path — one whose condition less half met — drawn **hollow block arrow**: thin grey perimeter outline white interior (rectangular shaft opening arrowhead), rather solid line. keeps merely available path blending faint small-magnitude *active* force, keeps solid colour-filled line. Conjunctive (dotted) edges exempt. Set `FALSE` draw every edge solid line. edge_labels Logical: label drawn edge resolved force level. Default `TRUE`. edge_label_fmt Function formatting resolved coefficient edge label. Default -leading-zero house style trailing zeros trimmed (`1`, `.9`, `-.1`). plan_legend Logical; `TRUE` (default) `plan` [planF()] object, draw **plan-list** upper-left corner: "Plan <label>" heading one line per step (`a1: prepare coffee machine`, ...), green ramp colour arrow, step whose policy arrow **active** frame — condition met, action agent intends next — shown **bold**. effect plain-string `plan`. plan_legend_x, plan_legend_y Data coordinates plan-list's top-left anchor. Default `NULL` — layout's upper-left (`min(x)`, `max(y)`), staircase layouts leave empty. plan_legend_size Text size plan-list. Default `3.2`. plan_legend_title Heading plan-list. Default `NULL` — `"Plan <label>"` `planF` object. active_min Minimum activation plan step's condition (`prod(s[condition])`) count **active** step — one bolded plan-list lit green diagram. what: Field-style path diagram for an X-M-Y model — plotPathXMY
title: Field-style path diagram for an X-M-Y model — plotPathXMY
text: Renders X M Y structure ggplot2 object using funfield visualization conventions: Nodes colored expected score X     held 1, continuous diverging gradient (deep red white     deep blue, midpoint 0) mapped [-1, 1] range     via ggplot2::scale_fill_gradient2(). X node     definition 1 (full blue); mediator's score path     coefficient \(b^1_{MX}\); Y's score sum direct     indirect contributions,     \(b^1_{YX} + \sum_j b^1_{YM_j} \cdot b^1_{MX_j}\). Shape defaults true square X     mediator (continuous variables) left-facing triangle Y     (focal action likelihood). X_shape Y_shape     override : use X_shape = "rtTri" X     imagined action (e.g. speeding / overtime EXSJTs),     Y_shape = "square" Y regular continuous variable     rather action likelihood. Mediators always squares. Labels sit outside shapes:     mediator (M), X Y. convention scales     fan view. Edges solid positive paths dashed     negative paths. Linewidth edge color scale path     magnitude (capped scale_max): largest paths     render thick near-black, paths coefficients near     zero fade thin, near-white line recede     background. Arrowheads clipped node perimeter via     shape-aware intersection (L-infinity squares, edge     intersection triangles). Edge labels carry     f1 + fZ(Z) decomposition. Moderator (Z) coloring. Anything purely     bZ coefficient renders gold (#F6BE00), matching     condition-node color used deductive plotField()     diagrams. default decomposed label, trailing     bZ(Z) fragment gold (b1 portion edge     describe normative path). Z-overlay view     (Z_overlay = TRUE), entire label edge gold,     since edge bZ coefficient. Z-collapsed view     (Z_value supplied) renders normally: label     single total slope f1 + fZ * Z_value, bZ coefficient     . Two view modes: Triangle (single mediator) – X bottom-left, M     top, Y bottom-right. Fan (multiple mediators) – X left, mediators     stacked middle, Y right. odd number     mediators stack laid asymmetrically straight     X Y arrow clean gap y = 0 axis. controls fit drives diagram: "loop" (default): per-mediator loop overlay. fan     view overlay intentionally pure – residual direct     X Y arrow drawn, since sensible direct path     picture come different (joint) fit.     single-mediator triangle view, X Y direct path     one mediator's loop residual. "joint": joint multi-mediator simultaneous-fit     picture – mediator's slopes partial slopes net     mediators, X Y arrow single global     f1_XY_joint direct path controlling M. Passing Z_overlay = TRUE renders layout B1 coefficient swapped BZ counterpart – clean view moderator's per-unit effect path weights, isolated normative field. Nodes drawn white view since expected scores meaning per-unit-Z slope. Passing route = "expectation" "valuation" renders per-route view moderation. expectation route shows fZ_XM X--M arm (gold) f1_MY M--Y arm (black); valuation route flips arms (f1_XM left, fZ_MY right). visual grammar coloring scheme makes two route components moderation decomposition read directly: arms mediator carry visible weight one two graphs, gold–black black–gold chain identifies reason moderator shifts X--Y relationship. Mutually exclusive Z_value Z_overlay; nodes drawn white route views reason Z overlay.

title: Speeding ESJT Dataset — speedingESJT

title: Speeding ESJT Dataset — speedingESJT
text: Data cleaning. raw dataset contained 347 participants. Three sequential data-quality screens applied: Straightlining (fancyr::strLine(), cut > 10%     (person, action) rows showing zero variance across 9 rated columns). Low overall variability (fancyr::prMaxSD(), cut     < 0.25 maximum possible SD across 27 rated cells). Response speed (fancyr::spi() combined across     four speeding rating blocks, cut < 1 second per item per Wood,     Harms, Lowman, & DeSimone, 2017). Respondents missing timing data (known intermittent Qualtrics recording failure) retained — missingness screen treated evidence drop. Final analytic sample: N = 333 participants. Rescaling. Likert items stored [0, 1] proportion-- maximum-possible (POMP) scale (fancyr::pomp(x, smin = 1, smax = 5)). Differences two actions [0, 1] outcome therefore fall [-1, 1], natural metric expectancy-value reasoning. Typical workflow. Within-person deviate Level-1 columns fitting path models: Scoring named trait composites. composite recipes $codebook$trait_scales reproduce named scales used Wood, Harms, & Cho (2023):
code: data(speedingESJT) L1 <- c("Speed","MoneyCost","FunDrive","IntQuality","Appropriate",         "OnTime","Crash","Injured","Ticket","Likelihood") dev <- speedingESJT$PSI for (v in L1) dev[[v]] <- ave(dev[[v]], dev$p,                               FUN = function(x) x - mean(x)) tr <- speedingESJT$traits tr$Extra <- rowMeans(cbind(1 - tr$SR_15, tr$SR_16, tr$SR_17), na.rm = TRUE) tr$Open  <- rowMeans(cbind(tr$SR_27, 1 - tr$SR_28, tr$SR_29), na.rm = TRUE) tr$SRFastDriver <- tr$SR_30
